# terrible_community_general_datadog_downtime (Resource)





<!-- schema generated by tfplugindocs -->
## Schema

### Required

- `api_key` (String) Your Datadog API key.
- `app_key` (String) Your Datadog app key.
- `host_id` (String) ID of the `terrible_host` to run this task against

### Optional

- `api_host` (String) The URL to the Datadog API. This value can also be set with the `DATADOG_HOST` environment variable.
- `async_seconds` (Number) Run the task asynchronously, timing out after this many seconds. 0 = synchronous (default).
- `changed_when` (String) Jinja2 expression that overrides when the task is considered changed (e.g. 'false').
- `delegate_to_id` (String) ID of another terrible_host to delegate execution to.
- `downtime_message` (String) A message to include with notifications for this downtime. Email notifications can be sent to specific users by using the same "@username" notation as events.
- `end` (Number) POSIX timestamp to end the downtime. If not provided, the downtime is in effect until you cancel it.
- `environment` (String) Environment variables set for the task (dict of nameâ†’value).
- `failed_when` (String) Jinja2 expression that overrides when the task is considered failed.
- `ignore_errors` (Boolean) When true, a failed task does not raise a Terraform error.
- `monitor_id` (Number) The ID of the monitor to mute. If not provided, the downtime applies to all monitors.
- `monitor_tags` (String) A list of monitor tags to which the downtime applies. The resulting downtime applies to monitors that match ALL provided monitor tags.
- `poll_interval` (Number) Polling interval in seconds when async_seconds > 0. Defaults to 15.
- `rrule` (String) The `RRULE` standard for defining recurring events. For example, to have a recurring event on the first day of each month, select a type of rrule and set the `FREQ` to `MONTHLY` and `BYMONTHDAY` to `1`. Most common rrule options from the iCalendar Spec are supported. Attributes specifying the duration in `RRULE` are not supported (for example `DTSTART`, `DTEND`, `DURATION`).
- `scope` (String) A list of scopes to which the downtime applies. The resulting downtime applies to sources that matches ALL provided scopes.
- `skip_tags` (String) Skip tasks with these Ansible tags (list of strings).
- `start` (Number) POSIX timestamp to start the downtime. If not provided, the downtime starts the moment it is created.
- `state` (String) The designated state of the downtime.
- `tags` (String) Run only tasks with these Ansible tags (list of strings).
- `timeout` (Number) Override the default execution timeout (seconds). Defaults to 300.
- `timezone` (String) The timezone for the downtime.
- `triggers` (String) Arbitrary map of values; any change triggers task re-execution

### Read-Only

- `changed` (Boolean) Whether the task reported a change
- `downtime` (String) The downtime returned by the API.
- `id` (String) Unique task resource ID
